message.content:
You are part of a behavior-preserving refactor that dissolves `Tools/rbk/rbgu_Utility.sh` into focused modules. Your job: edit `Tools/rbk/rbgo_OAuth.sh` ONLY. Two small sub-tasks. **NO logic changes** beyond moving in one function and fixing one call site.

Working dir: /Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_alpha_recipemuster (repo root). Do NOT run tabtargets/tests. Do NOT touch any file other than `Tools/rbk/rbgo_OAuth.sh`. `rbgu_Utility.sh` still exists; READ from it.

## Read first
- `Tools/rbk/rbgu_Utility.sh` (lines ~74-80, the stateless transient predicate).
- `Tools/rbk/rbgo_OAuth.sh` — your target. Note the "Base64 primitives" section (lines ~62-97) documented as "Stateless — no sentinel; safe to call from any module regardless of kindle order." Your moved-in function is the same kind of stateless cross-module helper and belongs near it.

## (a) ADD the transient-curl-status predicate, moved in from rbgu:
Copy `rbgu_curl_status_is_transient_predicate` (rbgu_Utility.sh lines ~74-80) VERBATIM, including its comment line `# Stateless — no sentinel; safe to call from any module regardless of kindle order.`, and rename it to `rbgo_curl_status_is_transient_predicate`. Place it adjacent to the rbgo base64 stateless-helper family. It has NO sentinel call (it is stateless) — do not add one. Do NOT change `zrbgo_kindle` or `zrbgo_sentinel` — rbgo's dependency set is unchanged (the predicate adds no state).

The body is exactly:
```
# Stateless — no sentinel; safe to call from any module regardless of kindle order.
rbgo_curl_status_is_transient_predicate() {
  case "${1:-}" in
    7|28|35|56) return 0 ;;
    *)          return 1 ;;
  esac
}
```

## (b) FIX rbgo's own existing call site:
`zrbgo_exchange_jwt_capture` currently calls `rbgu_curl_status_is_transient_predicate "${z_curl_status}"` (around line 210). Change that call to `rbgo_curl_status_is_transient_predicate "${z_curl_status}"` (now intra-module).

## Verify
After edits, `grep -n 'rbgu_\|ZRBGU_' Tools/rbk/rbgo_OAuth.sh` must return NOTHING (run it yourself, read-only). Report: where you placed the new function, the call-site fix, and the clean-grep confirmation.

attachment.content:
- cma-prep-pr: Prepare branch for upstream PR contribution
- rbk-prep-release: Prepare release candidate for upstream delivery
- vvc-BREAK-LOCK: Force-break a stuck VVC lock after a crash.
- vvc-commit: Guarded git commit
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

82	######################################################################

1	#!/bin/bash
2	#
3	# Copyright 2025 Scale Invariant, Inc.
4	#
5	# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
6	# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
7	# You may obtain a copy of the License at
8	#
9	#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
10	#
11	# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
12	# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
13	# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
14	# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
15	# limitations under the License.
16	#
17	# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
18	#
19	# Recipe Bottle Google OAuth - Service account JWT authentication
20	
21	set -euo pipefail
22	
23	# Multiple inclusion detection
24	test -z "${ZRBGO_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbgo multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
25	ZRBGO_SOURCED=1
26	
27	######################################################################
28	# Internal Functions (zrbgo_*)
29	
30	zrbgo_kindle() {
31	  test -z "${ZRBGO_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbgo already kindled"
32	
33	  # Validate required tools (rehomed from rbl_Locator.sh)
34	  command -v openssl >/dev/null 2>&1 || buc_die "openssl not found - required for JWT signing and encoding"
35	  command -v curl    >/dev/null 2>&1 || buc_die "curl not found - required for OAuth exchange"
36	  command -v jq      >/dev/null 2>&1 || buc_die "jq not found - required for JSON parsing"
37	
38	  buc_log_args "Ensure RBGC is kindled first"
39	  zrbgc_sentinel
40	
41	  buc_log_args "Check environment"
42	  zburd_sentinel
43	
44	  buc_log_args "Set Module Variables (ZRBGO_*)"
45	  readonly ZRBGO_JWT_HEADER_FILE="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgo_jwt_header.json"
46	  readonly ZRBGO_JWT_CLAIMS_FILE="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgo_jwt_claims.json"
47	  readonly ZRBGO_JWT_UNSIGNED_FILE="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgo_jwt_unsigned.txt"
48	  readonly ZRBGO_JWT_SIGNATURE_FILE="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgo_jwt_signature.txt"
49	  readonly ZRBGO_OAUTH_RESPONSE_FILE="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgo_oauth_response.json"
50	  readonly ZRBGO_OPENSSL_STDERR_FILE="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgo_openssl_stderr.txt"
51	  readonly ZRBGO_CURL_STDERR_FILE="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgo_curl_stderr.txt"
52	  readonly ZRBGO_JQ_STDERR_FILE="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgo_jq_stderr.txt"
53	  readonly ZRBGO_CONSUMER_RETRY_BODY_FILE="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgo_consumer_retry_body.json"
54	
55	  readonly ZRBGO_KINDLED=1
56	}
57	
58	zrbgo_sentinel() {
59	  test "${ZRBGO_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module rbgo not kindled - call zrbgo_kindle first"
60	}
61	
62	######################################################################
63	# Base64 primitives — generic openssl wrappers.
64	#
65	# Stateless — no sentinel; safe to call from any module regardless of
66	# kindle order. rbgo base64url-encodes JWT material; rbgg/rbgp decode SA
67	# private keys and rbfd decodes Cloud Build step outputs. The -A flag
68	# (suppress line wrapping) is load-bearing on every site and lives only
69	# here, so it cannot be silently dropped on one path.
70	
71	rbgo_base64_decode_string_to_file() {
72	  local -r z_b64="${1:-}"
73	  local -r z_output="${2:-}"
74	  test -n "${z_b64}"    || return 1
75	  test -n "${z_output}" || return 1
76	  printf '%s' "${z_b64}" | openssl enc -base64 -d -A > "${z_output}" || return 1
77	}
78	
79	rbgo_base64_decode_file_to_file() {
80	  local -r z_input="${1:-}"
81	  local -r z_output="${2:-}"
82	  test -n "${z_input}"  || return 1
83	  test -n "${z_output}" || return 1
84	  test -f "${z_input}"  || return 1
85	  openssl enc -base64 -d -A < "${z_input}" > "${z_output}" || return 1
86	}
87	
88	rbgo_base64_encode_string_capture() {
89	  local -r z_input="${1:-}"
90	  printf '%s' "${z_input}" | openssl enc -base64 -A
91	}
92	
93	rbgo_base64_encode_file_capture() {
94	  local -r z_file="${1:-}"
95	  test -f "${z_file}" || return 1
96	  openssl enc -base64 -A < "${z_file}"
97	}
98	
99	zrbgo_base64url_encode_capture() {
100	  zrbgo_sentinel
101	
102	  local -r z_input="${1:-}"
103	
104	  # Base64url: encode, then URL-safe transform and strip = padding
105	  local z_encoded
106	  z_encoded=$(rbgo_base64_encode_string_capture "${z_input}") || {
107	    buc_warn "openssl base64url encode failed"
108	    return 1
109	  }
110	  z_encoded="${z_encoded//+/-}"
111	  z_encoded="${z_encoded//\//_}"
112	  z_encoded="${z_encoded//=/}"
113	  printf '%s' "${z_encoded}"
114	}
115	
116	zrbgo_build_jwt_capture() {
117	  zrbgo_sentinel
118	
119	  local -r z_rbra_file="$1"
120	
121	  # RBRA_* expected: CLIENT_EMAIL, PRIVATE_KEY, TOKEN_LIFETIME_SEC
122	  # RBRA_PRIVATE_KEY is sourced as a multi-line string (real newlines from PEM).
123	  # printf '%b' below is defensive and tolerates either real-newline or '\n'-escape
124	  # form; PEM keys contain no backslashes so %b is otherwise a no-op.
125	  # Only source if not already loaded (avoids readonly conflict in subshells)
126	  if test -z "${RBRA_CLIENT_EMAIL:-}"; then
127	    buc_log_args "Source RBRA file"
128	    source "${z_rbra_file}" || return 1
129	  fi
130	
131	  buc_log_args "Validate required variables"
132	  test -n "${RBRA_CLIENT_EMAIL:-}"       || return 1
133	  test -n "${RBRA_PRIVATE_KEY:-}"        || return 1
134	  test -n "${RBRA_TOKEN_LIFETIME_SEC:-}" || return 1
135	
136	  buc_log_args "Build JWT header"
137	  printf '%s' '{"alg":"RS256","typ":"JWT"}' > "${ZRBGO_JWT_HEADER_FILE}"
138	
139	  buc_log_args "Calculate timestamps"
140	  local z_now
141	  z_now=$(date +%s) || return 1
142	  local -r z_exp=$((z_now + RBRA_TOKEN_LIFETIME_SEC))
143	
144	  buc_log_args "Build JWT claims"
145	  jq -n                                         \
146	    --arg iss   "${RBRA_CLIENT_EMAIL}"          \
147	    --arg scope "${RBGC_SCOPE_CLOUD_PLATFORM}"  \
148	    --arg aud   "${RBGC_OAUTH_TOKEN_URL}"       \
149	    --argjson iat "${z_now}"                    \
150	    --argjson exp "${z_exp}"                    \
151	    '{iss: $iss, scope: $scope, aud: $aud, iat: $iat, exp: $exp}' \
152	    > "${ZRBGO_JWT_CLAIMS_FILE}" || return 1
153	
154	  buc_log_args "Encode header and claims"
155	  local z_header_enc
156	  z_header_enc=$(zrbgo_base64url_encode_capture "$(<"${ZRBGO_JWT_HEADER_FILE}")") || return 1
157	
158	  local z_claims_enc
159	  z_claims_enc=$(zrbgo_base64url_encode_capture "$(<"${ZRBGO_JWT_CLAIMS_FILE}")") || return 1
160	
161	  buc_log_args "Create unsigned JWT"
162	  printf '%s' "${z_header_enc}.${z_claims_enc}" > "${ZRBGO_JWT_UNSIGNED_FILE}"
163	
164	  buc_log_args "Sign with RSA256 (without writing key to disk)"
165	  # Convert literal \n sequences to real newlines via printf %b and feed via process substitution
166	  openssl dgst -sha256 \
167	    -sign <(printf '%b' "${RBRA_PRIVATE_KEY}\n") \
168	    -out "${ZRBGO_JWT_SIGNATURE_FILE}" \
169	    "${ZRBGO_JWT_UNSIGNED_FILE}" 2>"${ZRBGO_OPENSSL_STDERR_FILE}" || {
170	      buc_warn "openssl RSA256 sign failed: $(<"${ZRBGO_OPENSSL_STDERR_FILE}")"
171	      return 1
172	    }
173	
174	  buc_log_args "Base64url encode signature"
175	  local z_signature
176	  z_signature=$(rbgo_base64_encode_file_capture "${ZRBGO_JWT_SIGNATURE_FILE}") || {
177	    buc_warn "openssl signature base64 encode failed"
178	    return 1
179	  }
180	  z_signature="${z_signature//+/-}"
181	  z_signature="${z_signature//\//_}"
182	  z_signature="${z_signature//=/}"
183	
184	  buc_log_args "Return complete JWT"
185	  printf '%s\n' "${z_header_enc}.${z_claims_enc}.${z_signature}"
186	}
187	
188	zrbgo_exchange_jwt_capture() {
189	  zrbgo_sentinel
190	
191	  local -r z_jwt="$1"
192	
193	  local z_attempt=0
194	  local z_curl_status=0
195	  while :; do
196	    z_attempt=$((z_attempt + 1))
197	
198	    buc_log_args "Exchange JWT for OAuth token (attempt ${z_attempt}/${RBGC_HTTP_TRANSIENT_RETRY_ATTEMPTS})"
199	    z_curl_status=0
200	    curl -sS -X POST "${RBGC_OAUTH_TOKEN_URL}"                                        \
201	      --connect-timeout "${RBCC_CURL_CONNECT_TIMEOUT_SEC}"                           \
202	      --max-time "${RBCC_CURL_MAX_TIME_SEC}"                                         \
203	      -H "Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded"                           \
204	      -d "grant_type=urn:ietf:params:oauth:grant-type:jwt-bearer&assertion=${z_jwt}" \
205	      > "${ZRBGO_OAUTH_RESPONSE_FILE}" 2>"${ZRBGO_CURL_STDERR_FILE}" || z_curl_status=$?
206	
207	    test "${z_curl_status}" -eq 0 && break
208	
209	    buc_warn "OAuth curl exit ${z_curl_status}: $(<"${ZRBGO_CURL_STDERR_FILE}")"
210	    rbgu_curl_status_is_transient_predicate "${z_curl_status}" || return 1
211	    test "${z_attempt}" -lt "${RBGC_HTTP_TRANSIENT_RETRY_ATTEMPTS}" || return 1
212	
213	    buc_step "OAuth token mint: transient curl ${z_curl_status}, retry ${z_attempt}/${RBGC_HTTP_TRANSIENT_RETRY_ATTEMPTS} in ${RBGC_HTTP_TRANSIENT_RETRY_SLEEP_SEC}s"
214	    sleep "${RBGC_HTTP_TRANSIENT_RETRY_SLEEP_SEC}"
215	  done
216	
217	  # Scrubber filters by field NAME, not value. Deliberate best-effort log hygiene:
218	  # explicitly deletes access_token/refresh_token, then drops any field whose key
219	  # matches token|secret|key|password (case-insensitive) — catches id_token,
220	  # client_secret, etc. If the OAuth provider ever returns a new secret-carrying
221	  # field whose name doesn't match this regex, the scrub would miss it; update
222	  # the regex here when that happens.
223	  buc_log_args "Debug: Show the actual response (minus secrets)"
224	  jq 'del(.access_token, .refresh_token)
225	      | with_entries(select(.key | test("token|secret|key|password"; "i") | not))' \
226	    "${ZRBGO_OAUTH_RESPONSE_FILE}" 2>"${ZRBGO_JQ_STDERR_FILE}" | buc_log_pipe || buc_log_args "OAuth response parsing failed"
227	
228	  buc_log_args "OAuth token exchange completed"
229	
230	  buc_log_args "Extract access token"
231	  local z_token
232	  z_token=$(jq -r '.access_token // empty' "${ZRBGO_OAUTH_RESPONSE_FILE}") || return 1
233	  test -n "${z_token}" || {
234	    buc_warn "OAuth response missing access_token; redacted response body follows:"
235	    jq 'with_entries(select(.key | test("token|secret|key|password"; "i") | not))' \
236	      "${ZRBGO_OAUTH_RESPONSE_FILE}" 2>"${ZRBGO_JQ_STDERR_FILE}" >&2 \
237	      || cat "${ZRBGO_OAUTH_RESPONSE_FILE}" >&2
238	    return 1
239	  }
240	
241	  printf '%s\n' "${z_token}"
242	}
243	
244	######################################################################
245	# External Functions (rbgo_*)
246	
247	rbgo_get_token_capture() {
248	  zrbgo_sentinel
249	
250	  local -r z_rbra_file="$1"
251	
252	  buc_doc_brief "Exchange service account credentials for OAuth2 access token (retries on JWT-propagation race)"
253	  buc_doc_param "rbra_file" "Path to RBRA credentials file containing RBRA_* variables"
254	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
255	
256	  buc_log_args "Validate RBRA file exists"
257	  test -f "${z_rbra_file}" || return 1
258	
259	  local z_attempt=0
260	  local z_elapsed=0
261	  local z_delay="${RBGC_SA_KEY_CONSUMER_RETRY_INITIAL_DELAY_SEC}"
262	  local z_jwt=""
263	  local z_token=""
264	  local z_status=0
265	  local z_err=""
266	  local z_err_desc=""
267	
268	  while :; do
269	    z_attempt=$((z_attempt + 1))
270	
271	    z_status=0
272	    z_jwt=$(zrbgo_build_jwt_capture "${z_rbra_file}") || z_status=$?
273	    test "${z_status}" -eq 0 || return 1
274	
275	    z_status=0
276	    z_token=$(zrbgo_exchange_jwt_capture "${z_jwt}") || z_status=$?
277	
278	    if test "${z_status}" -eq 0 && test -n "${z_token}"; then
279	      printf '%s\n' "${z_token}"
280	      return 0
281	    fi
282	
283	    # Discriminate failure: only the SA-propagation race shapes retry —
284	    #   `invalid_grant` + `Invalid JWT Signature.`         (fresh-key propagation lag)
285	    #   `invalid_grant` + `Invalid grant: account not found` (fresh-SA propagation lag)
286	    # Any other shape fails fast.
287	    z_err=$(jq -r '.error // ""'             "${ZRBGO_OAUTH_RESPONSE_FILE}" 2>"${ZRBGO_JQ_STDERR_FILE}") || z_err=""
288	    z_err_desc=$(jq -r '.error_description // ""' "${ZRBGO_OAUTH_RESPONSE_FILE}" 2>"${ZRBGO_JQ_STDERR_FILE}") || z_err_desc=""
289	
290	    test "${z_err}" = "invalid_grant" || return 1
291	    case "${z_err_desc}" in
292	      "Invalid JWT Signature."|"Invalid grant: account not found") ;;
293	      *) return 1 ;;
294	    esac
295	
296	    cp "${ZRBGO_OAUTH_RESPONSE_FILE}" "${ZRBGO_CONSUMER_RETRY_BODY_FILE}" || return 1
297	
298	    if test "${z_elapsed}" -ge "${RBGC_SA_KEY_CONSUMER_RETRY_BUDGET_SEC}"; then
299	      buc_warn "Token mint: SA propagation race timeout after ${z_elapsed}s (${z_attempt} attempts); last body: ${ZRBGO_CONSUMER_RETRY_BODY_FILE}"
300	      return 1
301	    fi
302	
303	    buc_step "Token mint: attempt ${z_attempt} hit SA propagation race (${z_err_desc}) at ${z_elapsed}s, sleep ${z_delay}s"
304	    sleep "${z_delay}"
305	    z_elapsed=$((z_elapsed + z_delay))
306	    z_delay=$((z_delay * 2))
307	    test "${z_delay}" -le "${RBGC_SA_KEY_CONSUMER_RETRY_MAX_DELAY_SEC}" || z_delay="${RBGC_SA_KEY_CONSUMER_RETRY_MAX_DELAY_SEC}"
308	  done
309	}
310	
311	# eof
312

content.text:
Now I have a clear picture. I'll make both edits:

**(a)** Insert the stateless predicate after the base64 section (after line 97, before `zrbgo_base64url_encode_capture`).
**(b)** Fix the call site at line 210.

input.description: Verify no rbgu_ or ZRBGU_ references remain in rbgo_OAuth.sh

content.text:
Clean. Summary:

**Placement (a):** `rbgo_curl_status_is_transient_predicate` was inserted immediately after `rbgo_base64_encode_file_capture` (the last of the four base64 primitives), before `zrbgo_base64url_encode_capture`. It sits in the stateless-helpers zone alongside the base64 family, with its `# Stateless — no sentinel; safe to call from any module regardless of kindle order.` comment line intact verbatim. No sentinel call added.

**Call-site fix (b):** `zrbgo_exchange_jwt_capture` line (previously ~210, now ~222 after insertion) — `rbgu_curl_status_is_transient_predicate` replaced with `rbgo_curl_status_is_transient_predicate`.

**Clean-grep confirmation:** `grep -n 'rbgu_\|ZRBGU_' Tools/rbk/rbgo_OAuth.sh` returned nothing.
